subject: feat(badges): Phase 4 — Timeline work-pattern badges to DaisyUI semantic variants
body: Fourth of 10 phases from the DaisyUI component-class audit. This phase eliminates the .badge + .badge-holiday / .badge-weekend / .badge-after-hours shadow of DaisyUI's built-in badge component. Changes: Timeline.jsx — three conditional work-pattern badges switched from Holiday to Holiday Mapping: isHoliday -> badge-accent (warm pink-ish — festive) isWeekend -> badge-info (cool blue — informational) isAfterHours (not weekend) -> badge-warning (amber — "attention") badge-sm keeps the footprint compact inside the commit metadata row where the badges appear alongside author/date/repo text. styles.css — deleted ~20 lines of custom badge CSS: - .badge (base rule: inline-flex, padding, radius, font-size, uppercase, letter-spacing) — was SHADOWING DaisyUI's .badge - .badge-after-hours (rgba-amber) - .badge-weekend (rgba-purple) - .badge-holiday (rgba-pink) Replaced with a one-line comment pointing at Timeline.jsx. Why this matters beyond Timeline: Our .badge rule was unlayered CSS that won every specificity contest against DaisyUI's @layer components badge. Deleting it means any future badge usage anywhere in the codebase (e.g. a new contributor role badge in Contributors section, status badges on Projects, etc.) will correctly get DaisyUI's component styles without having to remember that "we shadow .badge". One less footgun. Build verification: - npm run build: passes - .badge-holiday, .badge-weekend, .badge-after-hours confirmed GONE - DaisyUI .badge, .badge-accent, .badge-info, .badge-warning, .badge-sm confirmed present in built CSS Tags: ui, daisyui, refactor, badges Complexity: 1 Urgency: 1 Impact: user-facing Risk: low Debt: paid Epic: daisyui-component-migration Semver: patch https://claude.ai/code/session_018prUZYyhuCZQGS1azip9bG
title: Phase 4 — Timeline work-pattern badges to DaisyUI semantic variants
